invariants: The startup is a HAND-STACKED tower of two or three flat cream blocks, slightly imperfect and uneven, never a single block and never a neat machine-precise stack.
Cream wings are attached to the tower and the whole reads as LIFT: rising, climbing, or airborne, never sitting still on a surface.
Plate register only: flat solid terracotta ground, a single-weight wobbly confident black ink outline, flat ivory-cream fills as the only fills.
Absolutely no text, letters, numbers, UI, or cursor marks, ever.
Face-on and flat: no perspective, no isometric, no parallelograms, no side faces or edge thickness, no shading, no gradients, no coloured linework.

prose.voice: The campaign's central metaphor, its sign-off made visible: a startup that has been given wings. 'Fly with Claude.'
prose.lore: Recurs across the Build on Anthropic deck and campaign surfaces: the browser-tab favicon, the deck cover hero (a hand releasing it), and the closing 'help you fly' slide (it soaring free). Because it appears at 16px and at full-bleed, it must read as the same object every time.
prose.rules: Master-first: the hero is the canonical calm, centered, face-on winged block-tower on plain terracotta. Every other use (the tilted climbing favicon, the hand-released cover, the soaring close) is derived as its own render with the locked hero passed as the first input image, never re-invented from prose. A render whose block-tower or wings disagree with the hero is a defect: regenerate from the master.
